findings.description: Two-column layout is completely lost and content is duplicated/fragmented.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows a left column for 'AUDIO TROUBLESHOOTING' and a right column with a corkboard background for 'Welcome...'. The HTML renders these as a single column, first showing image snippets of the original columns, then duplicating the text content sequentially.

findings.description: Right column containing images is lost.
findings.evidence: The PDF has a main table section and a distinct right column with a vertical stack of five images. The HTML collapses this into a single column.

findings.description: Images from the right column are displaced and split across pages.
findings.evidence: The five images from the right column of PDF page 3 are placed sequentially in the HTML, with the first two appearing at the bottom of HTML page 2 and the remaining three at the top of HTML page 3.

findings.description: Reading order of images is altered.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the images are visually parallel to the table. In the HTML, they are placed before the 'Coastal Habitat Protection Plan' heading and table.
